task: The charset regex in get_encodings_from_content (utils.py) uses a non-greedy group. Simplify it to a plain greedy group.
known_issue: Changing (.+?) to (.+) makes the capture greedy: it now consumes up to the LAST quote/bracket on the line instead of stopping at the encoding token. For a meta tag like <meta charset='utf-8'> followed by other attributes or markup on the same line, the captured 'encoding' becomes a long garbage string rather than 'utf-8', so the detected encoding is wrong or unusable.
